description: Children reset devices; guess passcodes; change time zones or use web versions of blocked apps
residual_note: Controls are guardrails not walls; the workaround conversation matters more than the lock

risk_name: Stranger contact via Messages and FaceTime
description: Unknown numbers can reach a child unless communication limits are set
residual_note: Group chats added by known contacts can still include strangers

risk_name: Age rating gaps in the App Store
description: Apps sometimes carry ratings lower than their content warrants; especially chat-enabled apps
residual_note: Ask to Buy approval is your review moment; check what the app actually does

description: Children can opt out of Family Link at 13; parents are notified but control drops sharply
residual_note: Plan the 13th birthday conversation before the birthday; agree what continues voluntarily

description: APK files installed outside Google Play bypass every Play restriction
residual_note: Block unknown sources in device settings; watch for file-sharing apps

description: Step-by-step Family Link bypass guides circulate on YouTube and search
residual_note: Assume your child has seen them; ask what they know rather than pretending they have not

risk_name: Stranger voice and text chat in games
description: In-game chat exposes children to adults; grooming often starts with compliments and gift items
residual_note: Voice chat via phone apps like Discord sits outside console controls entirely

risk_name: One restriction level for all children
description: Switch controls are per console; siblings of different ages share the same limits
residual_note: Set for the youngest; use the PIN to unlock for the older child while supervised

description: Loot boxes; season passes and item shops apply constant engineered spending pressure
residual_note: Gift cards bypass caps; brief relatives who gift them

description: Crossplay connects children to unmoderated PC populations
residual_note: Friends-only multiplayer is the sensible middle before open crossplay

risk_name: Immersive harassment and adult spaces in VR
description: Social VR harassment lands harder than flat-screen equivalents; adult spaces are close by
residual_note: Keep sessions short and in shared rooms; casting makes the invisible visible

description: TV YouTube without a signed-in supervised account has no filters and full autoplay
residual_note: The TV is where the youngest children watch unsupervised most often

description: ISP filtering ends where 4G begins
residual_note: Device-level controls travel with the child; the router is one layer of several

description: Criminal networks target teens especially boys with fake profiles then blackmail over images
residual_note: The pattern moves off-platform fast; the protective script matters as much as the setting

description: Algorithmic feeds amplify idealised bodies and lifestyles; linked to mood and body dissatisfaction for some teens
residual_note: Evidence is contested on averages but clear enough on vulnerable subgroups; watch the child not the average

description: A finsta created with a false birthday carries no teen protections and no supervision
residual_note: Meta age-detection AI catches some; the honest conversation catches more

description: The For You feed can serve escalating content on dieting; self-harm adjacent themes and extreme material
residual_note: Restricted Mode reduces but does not eliminate; ask what the feed is showing them

description: When TikTok is limited the same short-form content flows through YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels
residual_note: Set limits by format not by app; the format is the compulsion

description: Vanishing chats create false safety; recipients screenshot and images persist
residual_note: No setting fixes this; the think-before-you-send script is the mitigation

description: Live location visible to a whole friends list including loosely known contacts
residual_note: None while Ghost Mode holds

description: Autoplay and recommendations drift from age-appropriate to borderline over a session
residual_note: No filter is perfect; co-viewing habits for the youngest do more than any toggle

description: Short-form autoplay feeds produce the longest unintended sessions
residual_note: The same format lives on three other platforms

description: Strangers or loose contacts add children to groups containing scams; bullying or explicit content
residual_note: A known contact can still add them to a bad group; teach leave and tell

description: Verification-code phishing hijacks accounts then targets the child's contacts
residual_note: The never-share-the-code rule is the mitigation

description: Predators build trust via compliments; Robux gifts and exclusive attention then move chat off-platform
residual_note: The off-platform move to Discord or Snapchat is the escalation signal; name it for your child

description: User-created games with sexual or violent content periodically evade moderation
residual_note: Report and block; the June 2026 account types cut exposure sharply for under 9s

description: Avatar items and game passes carry playground status; spending pressure is peer-driven
residual_note: Grandparent gift cards bypass caps

description: Squad fill matches put children in live voice with adult strangers by default culture
residual_note: Friends-only squads preserve the fun and cut the exposure

description: Discord is where conversations move when other platforms are locked down; servers are unmoderated by default
residual_note: Family Center shows who not what; the monthly server conversation is the real control

description: X allows pornography and has no parental controls; sensitive media settings are trivially changed
residual_note: No configuration makes X appropriate for under 16s; the guidance is the pathway not the toggle

description: Across all devices the best-evidenced harm channel is displaced sleep; movement and face-to-face time rather than content alone
residual_note: This is why the platform covers the full family screen picture not social media alone
